description: Performers, artists, musicians, and those who entertain.
jobs: Jester
A bell-capped trickster cracking jokes and mocking the powerful from beneath jingling motley.
Fool
A motley-clad truth-speaker disguising hard wisdom as nonsense for the court's amusement.
Minstrel
A road-worn singer wandering from inn to inn, strumming ballads on a battered lute.
Troubadour
A velvet-voiced composer of courtly love poems, performing before noble audiences.
Bard
A fire-lit weaver of ancient tales and legends, keeping history alive through song.
Musician
Calloused fingers playing strings, winds, or drums at feasts, fairs, and street corners.
Harper
A long-fingered player plucking shimmering melodies from a tall gilded harp.
Lute Player
A nimble picker of intricate melodies on a pear-shaped lute, at home in tavern or court.
Drummer
A sweat-sheened beater of animal-skin drums, setting the rhythm for marches and dances.
Piper
A red-cheeked player blowing lively tunes through wooden pipes to set feet tapping.
Singer
A powerful voice filling the hall alone, carrying melodies from hymns to drinking songs.
Dancer
A silk-footed performer spinning and gliding through choreographed steps for an admiring crowd.
Acrobat
A chalk-dusted body flipping through the air and balancing atop swaying human towers.
Juggler
Quick hands keeping daggers, torches, and colored balls spinning without a single drop.
Tumbler
A spring-legged performer cartwheeling and backflipping across the market square.
Fire Eater
A scorched-lipped daredevil tilting a blazing torch into an open mouth without flinching.
Sword Swallower
A throat-bared performer guiding a long blade slowly downward in nerve-racking display.
Puppeteer
A hidden hand pulling strings behind a painted booth, bringing wooden characters to life.
Storyteller
A bright-eyed narrator holding a crowd rapt with nothing but voice, gesture, and a well-told tale.
Mime
A white-faced performer telling entire stories using only gesture and expression.
Jongleur
A sweat-drenched showman juggling, singing, and tumbling -- switching acts to keep any crowd hooked.
Animal Trainer
A treat-pouch-wearing handler teaching dogs, monkeys, and horses to perform for cheering crowds.
Bear Trainer
A chain-gripping handler coaxing a muzzled bear to dance and wrestle for tossed coins.
Snake Charmer
A cross-legged player swaying with a reed pipe as a hooded cobra rises from its basket.
Street Performer
A cobblestone busker drawing a circle and entertaining passersby for tossed copper.
Court Musician
A finely dressed player performing refined compositions during royal meals and ceremonies.
Theater Actor
A greasepaint-smeared performer delivering lines and dying dramatically on a wooden stage.
Playwright
An ink-stained writer of tragedies, comedies, and histories for theater companies to stage.
Poet
A verse-crafting dreamer reciting meter and rhyme at court gatherings and coffeehouses.
Chronicler
A quill-worn recorder writing down the deeds of kings so future ages may remember.
Painter
A pigment-smudged artist brushing images onto wood panels and stretched canvas.
Portrait Artist
A detail-obsessed painter capturing a noble's likeness, spending hours on every shadow.
Composer
A notation-scratching creator of original melodies, filling staff paper with new harmonies.
Music Master
A stern-eared instructor drilling students in scales, timing, and technique.
Dancing Master
A posture-perfect teacher instructing courtiers in the steps of formal dances before the ball.
Dungeon Master
A torch-bearing warden managing prisoners and security in the castle's depths below ground.
Sign Painter
A brush-wielding letterer painting colorful trade signs that hang above shop doorways.
Tattoo Artist
A bone-needle artist pricking permanent ink designs into skin one careful dot at a time.
Body Painter
A pigment-fingered artist brushing temporary designs across skin for festivals and rites.
Race Organizer
A flag-dropping coordinator marking courses and lining up contestants for foot and horse races.
Festival Coordinator
A tireless planner of food, music, and games for community celebrations through the seasons.
Wedding Planner
A flower-arranging organizer coordinating ceremony, feast, and every detail of a marriage day.
Horn Blower
A red-faced signaler sounding a great horn from tower or hilltop for alarms and announcements.
Drum Beater
A cadence-pounding herald beating a field drum to signal troops or announce proclamations.
Circus Master
A top-hat-wearing ringmaster running a traveling show of acrobats, animals, and oddities.
Carnival Barker
A throat-straining pitchman shouting colorful come-ons to lure crowds into sideshow tents.
Sideshow Performer
A gasps-drawing exhibitor of rare physical feats that make audiences stare.
Strongman
A barrel-chested giant bending iron bars and snapping chains to amaze a cheering crowd.
Contortionist
A seemingly boneless performer folding into impossible shapes on stage.
Knife Thrower
A steel-nerved hurler of blades at a spinning board, outlining a partner without drawing blood.
Fire Breather
An oil-mouthed performer spraying fuel across a torch, sending a roaring plume of flame.
Tightrope Walker
A balance-pole-gripping daredevil stepping across a thin rope high above the gasping crowd.
Clown
A red-nosed stumbler pratfalling and squirting water to make children and adults alike laugh.
Buffoon
A wild-eyed fool earning laughs through exaggerated stumbles and theatrical stupidity.
Impressionist
A voice-shifting mimic copying the mannerisms of nobles and public figures for comic effect.
Ventriloquist
A still-lipped trickster throwing a voice into a wooden dummy that seems to speak alone.
Puppet Master
A string-pulling artist staging elaborate miniature dramas with complex marionettes.
Shadow Puppeteer
A lantern-lit storyteller casting moving cut-out figures onto a cloth screen.
Magic Lantern Operator
A slide-changing showman projecting painted glass images onto a wall to tell stories in light.
Peep Show Operator
A lens-polishing exhibitor of magnified painted scenes viewed one at a time through a box.
Theater Manager
A ledger-keeping impresario booking acts and keeping a playhouse running night after night.
Stage Manager
A curtain-pulling coordinator cueing entrances and keeping every scene change on schedule.
Costume Designer
A fabric-draped creator sketching and sewing costumes that bring each character to visual life.
Set Builder
A sawdust-covered carpenter hammering together painted flats and backdrops for the stage.
Props Master
A detail-tracking gatherer of every object actors handle during a production.
Prompter
A curtain-hidden whisperer feeding forgotten lines so the show never stops.
Understudy
A shadow performer memorizing every line, ready to step on stage at a moment's notice.
Chorus Member
A unison-moving singer and dancer supporting the lead performers as part of a troupe.
Orchestra Leader
A baton-waving timekeeper counting musicians in and holding the tempo steady.
Conductor
A gesture-sculpting maestro shaping the ensemble's sound with each sweep of the hand.
Lyricist
A word-fitting writer crafting verses that singers perform, matching syllable to melody.
Music Copyist
A neat-handed transcriber of musical scores, ensuring every player has a clean part to read.
Instrument Tuner
A pitch-perfect ear adjusting pegs, strings, and pipes until every note rings true.
Instrument Repairer
A glue-and-clamp worker mending cracked lute bodies and restringing worn instruments.
Music Teacher
A patient guide coaxing beginners through their first notes and correcting technique.
Dance Instructor
A rhythm-clapping demonstrator drilling students until partnered footwork turns smooth.
Choreographer
A formation-minded inventor of movement sequences, arranging dancers for performance.
Ballet Master
A barre-gripping taskmaster training dancers in precise classical technique.
Folk Dance Teacher
A tradition-keeping instructor keeping alive the circle dances and reels of the countryside.
Court Dancer
A graceful performer of formal dances at royal banquets and state occasions.
Street Dancer
A cobblestone-stomping performer drawing crowds with raw energy and pounding rhythm.
Ritual Dancer
A temple-trained mover performing sacred choreography during holy ceremonies and festivals.
Exotic Dancer
A veil-trailing performer of slow, hypnotic dances in dimly lit halls.
Belly Dancer
A coin-draped shimmerer rolling hips to rhythmic Eastern drums in lamplight.
Fire Dancer
A poi-spinning silhouette painting swooping arcs of flame against the night sky.
Sword Dancer
A blade-whirling artist performing a martial dance of speed and controlled precision.
Morris Dancer
A handkerchief-waving stamper clashing sticks in lively English folk tradition.
May Pole Dancer
A ribbon-weaving celebrant twisting colored streamers around a tall pole for spring.
Wedding Dancer
A joy-spreading leader of traditional dances at marriage feasts for good fortune.
Funeral Dancer
A solemn-stepping mourner honoring the dead through ritual movement and gesture.
Rain Dancer
A dust-footed ritualist performing sacred steps to call down rain on parched fields.
War Dancer
A fury-building stamper chanting before battle, rousing courage in the ranks.
Victory Dancer
A triumph-whirling celebrant leaping when the army returns bearing victory.
Harvest Dancer
A sheaf-crowned performer dancing at the autumn feast, giving thanks for a good crop.
Seasonal Celebrant
A bonfire-lighting organizer of solstice feasts, equinox rites, and midsummer revels.
Holiday Organizer
A garland-hanging planner of games, processions, and decorations for holy days.
Dog Trainer
A whistle-blowing handler teaching hounds and terriers to sit, guard, and perform tricks.
Monkey Trainer
A cap-tipping showman coaxing small primates into somersaulting routines for street crowds.
Dancing Bear Leader
A chain-holding handler parading a shuffling bear through town squares for tossed coins.
Puppet Show Operator
A squeaky-voiced performer working hand puppets behind a striped booth.
Peep Show Owner
A penny-charging exhibitor of painted miniature scenes viewed through a curtained box.
Curiosity Displayer
A tent-flap-lifting showman exhibiting taxidermy oddities and foreign artifacts for a fee.
